description:
You are a performance-tuning engineer who has just received two INI files that record the latest resource-usage thresholds of two in-house services.  
Your goal is to automate the audit of those files and produce a single, machine-readable report.

Starting state
1. Two configuration files already exist in /home/user/configs/  
   • /home/user/configs/appA.ini  
   • /home/user/configs/appB.ini  
2. The directory /home/user/bin/ exists and is writable by the current user but is empty.  
3. The directory /home/user/reports/ exists and is writable by the current user but is empty.

Task
A. Write a POSIX-compliant bash script called /home/user/bin/profile_parser.sh that:
   1. Scans every *.ini file directly inside /home/user/configs/ (no recursion).  
   2. For each file, extracts:  
      • the service name found under the [meta] section’s key name  
      • every section named exactly cpu, memory, and io  
      • the integer values of the keys threshold and limit that exist inside each of the three sections
   3. Computes a status value for every section according to this logic:  
      • CRITICAL  – when threshold ≥ limit  
      • ALERT     – when threshold ≥ 0.8 × limit but < limit  
      • OK        – otherwise
   4. Writes one line for every analysed section to /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log following the precise format:

      <service_id>|<section>|threshold=<value>|limit=<value>|status=<OK|ALERT|CRITICAL>

      where <service_id> is the [meta] name value converted to snake_case  
      (all lowercase, spaces replaced by a single underscore, all other characters unchanged).

      Examples of the correct shape of a log line (values are illustrative, not actual):  
      foo_service|cpu|threshold=75|limit=90|status=ALERT  
      bar_app|memory|threshold=2048|limit=2048|status=CRITICAL

   5. Produces lines in the order the INI files are processed (alphabetical file name order) and, within each file, in the natural section order cpu → memory → io.
   6. Truncates /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log on every run so the file only contains data from the latest execution.
   7. Exits with status code 0 on success and >0 on error (missing keys, malformed numbers, etc.).

B. Make the script executable by the current user.

C. Execute the script once so that /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log is populated.

D. Display the complete contents of /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log on the terminal so the result is visible to the reviewer.

Verification
The automated checker will assert all of the following:

1. /home/user/bin/profile_parser.sh exists and has the executable bit set for the owner.  
2. /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log exists and is a regular file.  
3. The log file contains exactly six non-empty lines, each ending with a single newline (Unix linefeed).  
4. Every line strictly matches the prescribed pattern:  
   ^[a-z0-9_]+\\|(cpu|memory|io)\\|threshold=[0-9]+\\|limit=[0-9]+\\|(status=OK|status=ALERT|status=CRITICAL)$  
5. For each service and section the status value is mathematically correct.  
6. The line order is: all lines for appA.ini (cpu, memory, io) followed by all lines for appB.ini (cpu, memory, io).

Tip
• Avoid non-standard utilities; awk, sed, grep, cut, printf and bash built-ins are available.  
• Ensure numbers are treated as integers (no floating-point requirements).  
• Snake_case conversion must preserve any digits or punctuation already present in the original name—only space characters are swapped for underscores and letters are lower-cased.

This is a pure shell task; do not modify the existing INI files. Work entirely in /home/user. Good luck!
truth:
Initial files (already present BEFORE the agent starts):

File: /home/user/configs/appA.ini
------------------------------------------------
[meta]
name = Application Alpha
version = 1.2.3

[cpu]
threshold = 75
limit = 90

[memory]
threshold = 1024
limit = 2048

[io]
threshold = 120
limit = 150
------------------------------------------------

File: /home/user/configs/appB.ini
------------------------------------------------
[meta]
name = Beta Service
version = 4.5.6

[cpu]
threshold = 85
limit = 80

[memory]
threshold = 1500
limit = 1400

[io]
threshold = 100
limit = 200
------------------------------------------------

Expected file created by the agent:

File: /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log  
Exact content (including final newline):

application_alpha|cpu|threshold=75|limit=90|status=ALERT
application_alpha|memory|threshold=1024|limit=2048|status=OK
application_alpha|io|threshold=120|limit=150|status=ALERT
beta_service|cpu|threshold=85|limit=80|status=CRITICAL
beta_service|memory|threshold=1500|limit=1400|status=CRITICAL
beta_service|io|threshold=100|limit=200|status=OK

Permissions that will be verified:
• /home/user/bin/profile_parser.sh → mode 700 or 750 or 755 (owner executable is mandatory).  
• /home/user/reports/ini_audit.log → mode 600–666; only regular file status is required.

The checker will launch /home/user/bin/profile_parser.sh in a clean shell to ensure it exits with code 0 and re-generates the exact log above.
